checks.a_analysis: I have compared the source PDF with the output JSON content. Every heading and sentence in the output directly corresponds to content in the source document. No fabricated body text or headings were found. AI-generated elements like the title, table caption, and table summary are within acceptable guidelines.

All contact information including phone numbers (828-258-3939, 877-626-3198), passcode (8186681), the USFWS office address, and the WebEx URL were cross-checked. Every character and digit matches the source document exactly.
The source document is a single-page meeting agenda. The output captures all sections including the header, location details, remote access information, WebEx details, and the full agenda list.
The agenda in the source document consists of 7 time-stamped items. The pipeline has correctly converted this list into a structured table with 7 rows, maintaining 100% row fidelity.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section was reviewed for completeness. All details within the location, remote access, and agenda items are present. No paragraphs or key details were truncated or omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document. Formatting choices such as making labels like 'Date:' bold or organizing the agenda list into a table are acceptable structural normalization and do not alter the meaning of the information.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any duplicated text blocks. Each section of the meeting agenda and logistics is represented exactly once, mirroring the source document's content.

The document uses Level 2 headings for the main title and all primary sections ('Remote access if needed:', 'WebEx:', 'Agenda:'). While the document lacks an explicit H1 in the elements list (the title is in metadata), the hierarchy is flat and consistent, avoiding skips between levels. This is acceptable for accessibility normalization.

The agenda items, which appear as a two-column list in the source, have been correctly converted into a structural table. The columns correctly separate the meeting times from the activity descriptions, and the row count (7) matches the source content.
The reading order in the output JSON follows the top-to-bottom flow of the source PDF, starting with the title and logistics, followed by access information, and ending with the agenda table.

The agenda items are presented as a list in the source. In the JSON, these have been converted into a table format. This is an acceptable structural choice to preserve the alignment between timestamps and descriptions.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The JSON contains 4 headings. The HTML metadata indicates 5 headings in the rendered output. This difference of 1 heading is well within the allowed threshold (the larger of 10% or 3 headings), likely due to the renderer promoting the document title metadata to an H1.
The external WebEx URL is correctly identified as a link in the JSON and includes the 'opens_new_window' property. No incorrect or fabricated links were identified.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The metadata (Date, Location, Remote Access, WebEx) is grouped at the top, and the Agenda follows. The conversion of the agenda from a text list to a two-column table is a logical layout improvement for this type of structured information. A table of contents has been added, which is an expected navigational element in HTML conversion.

The source PDF does not contain formal tables, but lists structured information (the Agenda). The HTML conversion renders this agenda as a table. The resulting table is visually sound, with clear row separation, proper alignment between times and event descriptions, and legible content. This is a valid and helpful interpretation of the source's visual intent.
The source PDF consists entirely of text and does not contain any images, logos, or charts. No images are missing or broken in the HTML output.

There are no major blocks of content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) duplicated in the HTML output. While the title 'Federal Interagency Committee Agenda' appears both as a main page title and as a subheading for the table, this is a minor structural repetition common in accessible document conversion and does not constitute a defect in visual flow.

The overall visual quality of the HTML is high. Text is well-spaced, sections are clearly defined with headings, and the table conversion is clean. There is no evidence of text running together or excessive whitespace that would degrade readability or professionalism.
